summary: Markets rallied Friday as Iran peace talks sparked optimism, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hitting fresh records. Intel's massive Q1 earnings beat, driven by AI data center demand, sent its stock surging 21% and lifted AMD 13% in sympathy, fueling a semiconductor boom that pushed the iShares Semiconductor ETF to its 17th consecutive positive session. Bitcoin ETF inflows accelerated, with eight straight days totaling over $2 billion, the longest streak since October 2025, while BlackRock's IBIT options open interest surpassed Deribit's for the first time. The FOMC meeting on April 29 looms as Powell's final decision before his May exit, with futures pricing a 99.5% probability of holding rates at 3.50-3.75% amid persistent inflation and geopolitical uncertainty. Q1 earnings season shows 84% of S&P 500 companies beating EPS estimates, with blended growth at 15.1%, though firms like GE Aerospace and Eli Lilly are holding guidance despite strong results, citing macroeconomic and conflict-related headwinds.

signals.title: Intel Q1 earnings crush estimates, AI data center demand surges
signals.desc: Intel reported double-digit server CPU unit growth driven by AI infrastructure, sending its stock up 21% and lifting AMD 13% as analysts upgraded the sector.

signals.title: Bitcoin ETF inflows hit $2B over 8-day streak, BlackRock IBIT dominates
signals.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs logged eight consecutive days of inflows totaling $2 billion, the longest streak since October 2025, with IBIT absorbing 91% of flows.

signals.title: Powell's final FOMC meeting April 29 expected to hold rates at 3.50-3.75%
signals.desc: CME FedWatch shows 99.5% probability of a hold as Powell's last meeting before Warsh takes over in May, with no rate cuts expected amid elevated inflation.

signals.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit all-time highs on Iran peace talk optimism
signals.desc: Iran's foreign minister traveled to Pakistan for U.S. talks, lifting equities and sending oil down 2.4% as investors priced in de-escalation.

signals.title: Eli Lilly drops 4% on weak launch data for GLP-1 pill Foundayo
signals.desc: First full-week prescription data showed soft uptake for Lilly's oral GLP-1 versus Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, pressuring shares and raising competitive concerns.

verdict: Risk-on flows continue as peace talks offset inflation concerns and Powell's April 29 hold sets stage for post-FOMC earnings volatility from mega-cap tech.
